enrichmentSource: local Domain Intelligence Engine only (pattern matched: Record-Lifecycle Workflow)
source: Structured requirement scope. Business workflow is primary; UI and automation support it.

businessWorkflow.rule: Implement these first. They define the real business process and state transitions.

domainAwareRequirements.title: Create HumanResources Record (Human Resources)
domainAwareRequirements.businessReason: Supports the Record-Lifecycle Workflow business pattern for "Human Resources": Create HumanResources Record is a mandatory lifecycle step - without it the module cannot transition to the next state.
validations: required fields must be present before submission
status transitions must follow Draft -> Submitted -> Approved/Rejected -> Archived
only the owner or an admin may edit a record while in Draft
a rejected record requires a reason before the owner can resubmit
domainAwareRequirements.acceptanceCriteria: Owner can perform "Create HumanResources Record" only when business rules and validations for HumanResources are satisfied; result is reflected in status and audit trail.
edgeCases: Create HumanResources Record attempted with missing/invalid required fields
Create HumanResources Record attempted by a role without permission
Create HumanResources Record attempted on a record in an incompatible status
testScenarios: Positive: Create HumanResources Record succeeds with valid data and authorized role
Negative: Create HumanResources Record rejected with invalid data
Access: Create HumanResources Record rejected for unauthorized role
domainAwareRequirements.traceability: Record-Lifecycle Workflow -> Human Resources -> Create HumanResources Record

uiContract.rule: Use these to design pages, navigation, forms, tables, actions, and layout around the business workflow.

supportingUi.rule: These are supporting details. Do not let them outrank the business workflow.

automation.rule: Use this layer for API/data integration and tests that prove the business workflow.

release.rule: Use this layer for evidence, audit, reports, and signoff expectations.

traceability.note: Every completed build must map these IDs to files, APIs, tests, and evidence.
